arch-depth-recurrence-iteration-embeddin.atomic_change: Test one recurrence design at a time: shared blocks plus iteration embeddings or per-pass normalization under fixed width and budget.
arch-depth-recurrence-iteration-embeddin.title: Depth recurrence with iteration awareness
arch-depth-recurrence-iteration-embeddin.why_it_matters: Recurrence remains a live architecture lane, but the public evidence is mixed enough that it should stay atomic and controlled.

ctx-seq4096-train-eval.atomic_change: Run a controlled `seq_len=4096` lane with matching evaluation to test whether fewer, richer steps beat shorter contexts locally.
ctx-seq4096-train-eval.title: Long-context seq4096 train/eval lane
ctx-seq4096-train-eval.why_it_matters: Multiple PRs use `seq4096` and long-context evaluation as a strong baseline improvement path.

eval-document-isolated-sliding-window.atomic_change: Measure flat-stream eval versus per-document eval with sliding windows before adding any evaluation-time adaptation.
eval-document-isolated-sliding-window.title: Document-isolated sliding-window accounting
eval-document-isolated-sliding-window.why_it_matters: PR #77's ablation suggests much of the gain comes from document isolation and windowing before TTT itself.

eval-document-reset-ttt.atomic_change: Starting from document-isolated sliding-window eval, add score-before-update TTT with resets between documents using the smallest practical adapter or parameter subset.
eval-document-reset-ttt.title: Document-reset TTT / dynamic-eval lane
eval-document-reset-ttt.why_it_matters: The dynamic-eval memo and PR #77 both support reset-per-document adaptation as a real lane, but one that should stay separate from pure eval-accounting gains.

eval-sliding-window-context-accounting.atomic_change: Compare standard non-overlapping eval against stride-based sliding-window eval under fixed checkpoints.
eval-sliding-window-context-accounting.title: Sliding-window context accounting
eval-sliding-window-context-accounting.why_it_matters: Public PRs repeatedly show that sliding windows move BPB materially with zero artifact-cost changes.

init-ntk-overtone-rope.atomic_change: Test overtone-style embedding init, residual-mix initialization, and NTK-aware RoPE scaling independently before stacking.
init-ntk-overtone-rope.title: Initialization and NTK-aware RoPE lane
init-ntk-overtone-rope.why_it_matters: PR #60 suggests there may still be cheap initialization and eval-context wins outside the dominant sliding-window recipes.

quant-selective-int6-fp16-embed.atomic_change: Try int6 on large weight matrices while keeping the tied embedding or logit-sensitive tensors at higher precision.
quant-selective-int6-fp16-embed.title: Selective int6 plus fp16 embedding passthrough
quant-selective-int6-fp16-embed.why_it_matters: Several PRs point to selective precision, not uniform quantization, as the practical near-term gain.

quant-ternary-qat.atomic_change: Build one narrow ternary QAT lane and test whether the compression win survives practical training and evaluation under challenge constraints.
quant-ternary-qat.title: Ternary QAT stress test
quant-ternary-qat.why_it_matters: Ternary QAT is still high-upside but not yet grounded enough to be the repo default.

tok-vocab-scaling.atomic_change: Compare `sp1024` against larger vocab variants such as `sp4096` and `sp8192` with explicit BPB accounting.
tok-vocab-scaling.title: Tokenizer and vocabulary scaling
tok-vocab-scaling.why_it_matters: The frontier now shows real BPB improvements from larger vocabularies, and tokenizer cost accounting is still a live rules question.

tooling-local-sweeps-and-autoresearch.atomic_change: Create a trustworthy local proxy and let agentic or scripted sweeps explore only one controlled subspace at a time.
tooling-local-sweeps-and-autoresearch.title: Local sweep tooling and autoresearch ingestion
tooling-local-sweeps-and-autoresearch.why_it_matters: PRs #66 and #80 show that better local tooling can change iteration speed even when absolute BPB is not competitive yet.
